title: illigera cordata
intro: Illigera cordata, also known as the Indian plume, is a climbing plant native to the tropical forests of Asia, belonging to the family Hernandiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. The herbal preparations of Illigera cordata include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
